content.full_text:
CAL-SPAN
THE CALIFORNIA CHANNEL

December 10, 1990


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814-0814

To Whom it May Concern:

I am writing to seek formal advice as a follow up to a phone conversation on
December 7th with an FPPC representative. At that time, I asked if it was
still necessary for The California Channel to register as a Lobbyist
Employer and me to register as a lobbyist for 1991. The California Channel
is a statewide public affairs television network that would distribute the
video signal generated by the Legislature via satellite to cable operators.
More information regarding previous correspondence between The
California Channel and the FPPC may be obtained by looking at your file
number A-90-121.

In 1990, I met with state legislators to seek their support for the concept of
televising legislative sessions. This past week, the Assembly Rules
Committee appropriated funds to begin televising their sessions.

I anticipate that the remainder of my conversations with legislators and
staff will involve policy and technical issues. These issues should not
require a legislative or administrative action. In lieu of these recent
developments, do I still need to register as a lobbyist and file quarterly
reports?

Sincerely,

Paul Koplin
Executive Director


2121 Cloverfield Blvd., Suite 204 • Santa Monica, California 90404 • 213 315 1303 • FAX 213 829 9226

☒ LOBBYIST EMPLOYER REGISTRATION STATEMENT
☐ LOBBYING COALITION REGISTRATION STATEMENT

(Government Code Section 86105)

1991- 1992 Session
(Insert Years)

FORM 603
1990

TYPE OR PRINT IN INK

FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY

For information required to be provided to you pursuant to the Information Practices Act of
1977, see Information Manual on Lobbying Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act.

A

NAME OF LOBBYIST EMPLOYER OR LOBBYING COALITION:

TELEPHONE NUMBER:

The California Channel

(213) 306-2515

BUSINESS ADDRESS: (Number and Street)

(City)

(State)

(Zip Code)

2121 Cloverfield Blvd. Ste. 204, Santa Monica, CA 90404

If this is an Initial Registration, enter
the DATE QUALIFIED as a Lobbyist
Employer or Lobbying Coalition:

MAILING ADDRESS: (If different than above)

PART I - LOBBYISTS AND LOBBYING FIRMS EMPLOYED (List the full name of each in-house lobbyist employed and each
lobbying firm with which you contract.)

In-House Employee Lobbyists:

Lobbying Firms:

Paul Koplin

The California Channel (complete)

For each in-house employee lobbyist, attach a Form 604 (Lobbyist Certification) completed by the lobbyist and a $25 registration fee.

☐ If more space is needed, check box and attach continuation sheets.

PART II - STATE AGENCIES WHOSE ACTIONS YOU WILL ATTEMPT TO INFLUENCE

Legislature:

☒ Yes

☐ No

OTHER STATE AGENCIES (List)

☐ If more space is needed, check box and attach continuation sheets.

PART III - DESCRIPTION OF LOBBYING INTERESTS (See the instructions on the back of this form or the "Information Manual
on Lobbying Disclosure Provisions" for a discussion of how to report lobbying interests.)

Discussions with legislators and staff regarding policy issues on
televising the legislature.

VERIFICATION

I have used all reasonable diligence in preparing this Statement. I have reviewed the Statement and to the best of
my knowledge the information contained herein is true and complete.

I certify under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California that the foregoing is true and correct.

C

Executed on (Date)

At (City and State)

By (Signature of Responsible Officer)

D

Dec. 12, 1990

Santa Monica, CA

E

Name of Responsible Officer (Type or Print)

Title

Paul H. Koplin

Executive Director

F

NAME OF FILER: _____The California Channel_____

NATURE AND INTERESTS OF FILER (See instructions on reverse.)

Check ONE box ONLY:

☐ INDIVIDUAL (Complete only Parts A and E)
☐ BUSINESS ENTITY (Complete only Parts B and E)
☐ INDUSTRY, TRADE OR PROFESSIONAL ASSN. (Complete only Parts C and E)
☒ OTHER (Complete only Parts D and E)

A. INDIVIDUAL

1. NAME AND ADDRESS OF EMPLOYER
(or principal place of business if self-employed)

E. INDUSTRY GROUP CLASSIFICATION

(Check one box which most accurately describes the industry group which you represent. See instructions on reverse.)

☐ AGRICULTURE

2. DESCRIPTION OF BUSINESS ACTIVITY IN WHICH YOU OR YOUR EMPLOYER ARE ENGAGED

☒ EDUCATION

☐ GOVERNMENT

☐ HEALTH

B. BUSINESS ENTITY

DESCRIPTION OF BUSINESS ACTIVITY IN WHICH ENGAGED

☐ LABOR UNIONS

☐ LEGAL

☐ MISCELLANEOUS

C. INDUSTRY, TRADE OR PROFESSIONAL ASSN.

1. DESCRIPTION OF INDUSTRY, TRADE, OR PROFESSION REPRESENTED

☐ PUBLIC EMPLOYEES

☐ POLITICAL ORGANIZATIONS

☐ UTILITIES

2. SPECIFIC DESCRIPTION OF ANY PORTION OR FACTION OF THE INDUSTRY, TRADE, OR PROFESSION WHICH THE ASSOCIATION EXCLUSIVELY OR PRIMARILY REPRESENTS

BUSINESS: ☐ ENTERTAINMENT/RECREATION
☐ FINANCE/INSURANCE
☐ LODGING/RESTAURANTS
☐ MISCELLANEOUS
☐ MANUFACTURING/INDUSTRIAL
☐ MERCHANDISE/RETAIL
☐ OIL AND GAS
☐ PROFESSIONAL/TRADE
☐ REAL ESTATE
☐ TRANSPORTATION

3. NUMBER OF MEMBERS IN ASSOCIATION (Check appropriate box)

☐ 50 OR LESS (Provide names of all members on an attachment)
☐ MORE THAN 50

D. OTHER

1. STATEMENT OF NATURE AND PURPOSES

Creation of nonprofit public affairs television network

2. DESCRIPTION OF ANY TRADE, PROFESSION, OR OTHER GROUP WITH A COMMON ECONOMIC INTEREST WHICH IS PRINCIPALLY REPRESENTED OR FROM WHICH MEMBERSHIP OR FINANCIAL SUPPORT IS PRINCIPALLY DERIVED

LOBBYIST CERTIFICATION STATEMENT
(Government Code Section 86103)

1991- 1992 Session
(Insert Years)

FORM 604
1991/92

TYPE OR PRINT IN INK

FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY

For information required to be provided to you pursuant to the Information Practices Act of
1977, see Information Manual on Lobbying Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act.

TELEPHONE NUMBER:
( 213) 315-1303

NAME OF LOBBYIST: (Last) (First) (M.I.)
Koplin Paul H

BUSINESS ADDRESS: (Number and Street) (City) (State) (Zip Code)
2121 CLOVERFIELD BLVD, STE. 204, SANTA MONICA, CA 90403

If this is an initial Certification enter
the DATE QUALIFIED as a Lobbyist:

MAILING ADDRESS: (If different from above)

NAME OF LOBBYIST EMPLOYER OR LOBBYING FIRM:
The California Channel

PART I. BY SIGNING THE VERIFICATION BELOW, I CERTIFY THAT I HAVE READ AND
UNDERSTAND THAT I AM SUBJECT TO THE PROHIBITIONS CONTAINED IN
GOVERNMENT CODE SECTIONS 86203 AND 86205. (Provided on reverse)

PART II. PLEASE READ AND CHECK THE APPLICABLE BOX: .
(must check one box)

☐ I am aware of the requirement to attend an orientation course on the relevant ethical issues
and laws relating to lobbying. Upon receiving notice of the dates on which the courses will
be held, I will, within a reasonable time (to be determined by the Fair Political Practices
Commission), attend the course.

☒ I have completed the required orientation course on ethical issues and laws relating to
lobbying. (Ch. 84; Stats. of 1990)

IMPORTANT: If this is an Initial Certification or Renewal of Certification, attach a 3-inch by 4-inch black
and white photograph of yourself.

VERIFICATION

I have used all reasonable diligence in preparing this Statement. I have reviewed the Statement and to the best of
my knowledge the information contained herein is true and complete.

I certify under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California that the foregoing is true and correct.

C
Executed on (Date) At (City and State)

D DEC 12, 1990 Santa Monica, CA

E By (Signature of Lobbyist)

F [illegible signature]

CAL-SPAN                                                                                                                    THE CALIFORNIA CHANNEL

                                            Dec 11 4 08 PM '90

December 12, 1990


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814-0814

To Whom it May Concern:

Attached is a letter detailing the reasons why The California Channel may
not be required to file as a lobbyist employer and Paul Koplin as a lobbyist for
1991.

Pending judgement by the FPPC, we have completed the paperwork and
include a check for $25. Should The California Channel not be required to
register, we are requesting a full refund of the $25.

Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Patricia Bergstedt
Administrative Assistant




2121 Cloverfield Blvd., Suite 204 • Santa Monica, California 90404 • 213 315 1303 • FAX 213 829 9226

embedding.qa_text: December 10, 1990 Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. Box 807 428 J Street Sacramento, CA 95814-0814 To Whom it May Concern: I am writing to seek formal advice as a follow up to a phone conversation on December 7th with an FPPC representative. At that time, I asked if it was still necessary for The California Channel to register as a Lobbyist Employer and me to register as a lobbyist for 1991. The California Channel is a statewide public affairs television network that would distribute the video signal generated by the Legislature via satellite to cable operators. More information regarding previous correspondence between The California Channel and the FPPC may be obtained by looking at your file number A-90-121. In 1990, I met with state legislators to seek their support for the concept of televising legislative sessions. This past week, the Assembly Rules Committee appropriated funds to begin televising their sessions. I anticipate that the remainder of my conversations with legislators and staff will involve policy and technical issues. These issues should not require a legislative or administrative action. In lieu of these recent developments, do I still need to register as a lobbyist and file quarterly reports? Sincerely, [Signature] Paul Koplin Executive Director This form is a Lobbyist Employer Registration Statement for the year 1990, as indicated by FORM 603. The entity registering is "The California Channel," with its business address at 2121 Cloverfield Blvd., Ste. 204, Santa Monica, CA 90404. The telephone number listed is (213) 306-2515. PART I lists an in-house employee lobbyist named Paul Koplin and the lobbying firm employed, which is "The California Channel (Employee)." PART II indicates that there are no state agencies whose actions the entity will attempt to influence, as both checkboxes for "Yes" and "No" are marked. PART III describes the lobbying interests: "Discussions with legislators and staff regarding policy issues on televising the legislature." The verification section states that all reasonable diligence was used in preparing the statement, and the information contained therein is true and complete. It is signed by Paul H. Koplin, Executive Director, on Dec. 12, 1990, in Santa Monica, CA. NAME OF FILER: The California Channel NATURE AND INTERESTS OF FILER (See instructions on reverse.) Check ONE box ONLY: [ ] INDIVIDUAL (Complete only Parts A and E) [ ] BUSINESS ENTITY (Complete only Parts B and E) [ ] INDUSTRY, TRADE OR PROFESSIONAL ASSN. (Complete only Parts C and E) [X] OTHER (Complete only Parts D and E) A. INDIVIDUAL 1. NAME AND ADDRESS OF EMPLOYER (or principal place of business if self-employed) 2. DESCRIPTION OF BUSINESS ACTIVITY IN WHICH YOU OR YOUR EMPLOYER ARE ENGAGED B. BUSINESS ENTITY DESCRIPTION OF BUSINESS ACTIVITY IN WHICH ENGAGED C. INDUSTRY, TRADE OR PROFESSIONAL ASSN. 1. DESCRIPTION OF INDUSTRY, TRADE, OR PROFESSION REPRESENTED 2. SPECIFIC DESCRIPTION OF ANY PORTION OR FACTION OF THE INDUSTRY, TRADE, OR PROFESSION WHICH THE ASSOCIATION EXCLUSIVELY OR PRIMARILY REPRESENTS 3. NUMBER OF MEMBERS IN ASSOCIATION (Check appropriate box) [ ] 50 OR LESS
